design_philosophy.core_loop: Ethical decision-making and virtue cultivation through exploration, NPC interaction, shrine meditation, and morality-driven character development rather than combat-focused progression
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Internal moral struggle to achieve enlightenment across Eight Virtues while actions constantly risk degrading virtue standing; tension between player agency in ethical choices and mechanical consequences
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a spiritual leader and moral exemplar (Avatar) in a peaceful, prosperous world through righteous living rather than defeating evil
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Radical departure from traditional RPG evil-defeating narrative; rejects hack-and-slash dungeon crawl in favor of ethics-based progression; story focuses on personal spiritual improvement rather than external threats; virtue system means 'correct' playstyle is subjective and determined by player's own moral ranking of virtues

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through virtue system; player's ethical choices directly impact character capabilities and NPC interactions, allowing self-determined difficulty through moral decisions

progression.upgrade_system: Virtue enlightenment system: eight shrines scattered across Britannia, each requiring corresponding virtue rune and correct mantra repetition; meditation and in-game actions improve or degrade virtue standing; character class determined by initial virtue prioritization
progression.unlock_mechanism: Virtue runes required to enter shrines; artifacts required alongside virtue achievement to access Stygian Abyss; NPC interaction gated by virtue levels
replayability_hooks: Eight distinct virtue prioritizations create different character archetypes (Bard, Paladin, Tinker, etc.)
Multiple solutions for virtue improvement allow different playstyle approaches
NPC interactions vary based on player virtue standing
Actions carry moral weight affecting progression path

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through exploration of Renaissance Italy/Camelot-inspired Britannia
NPC dialogue system where interactions change based on player virtue
Gypsy fortune-teller tarot-like ethical dilemma system for character creation
Seer Hawkwind provides narrative feedback on virtue progress and moral guidance
Shrine meditation mechanics embed narrative through mechanical spiritual practice
Mantra repetition creates narrative immersion in virtue understanding

faction_indicators: Eight Virtues as ideological framework (Truth, Love, Courage, Compassion, Honor, Sacrifice, Spirituality, Humility)
Avatar status as ultimate social/spiritual achievement
Class system derived from virtue prioritization creates implicit social hierarchies
danger_signals: Virtue degradation through bragging or cowardice represents moral corruption
NPC reaction variation based on virtue could indicate social ostracism for vice
Straying from Avatar path explicitly framed as dangerous by Hawkwind
environmental_storytelling: Sixteen times larger overworld map than predecessor enables world exploration
Scattered shrines create pilgrimage narrative across geography
Cities built to replace lost ones after Triad of Evil defeat signal civilization recovery
Lord British's castle as narrative anchor and wisdom source
Stygian Abyss as final trial location with cosmological significance

sources_used: wikipedia: Ultima IV: Quest of the Avatar
